title: Gas prices keep climbing, and Massachusetts may see some of the biggest hikes
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Gas prices in Massachusetts rose by 50 cents per gallon due to disruptions from the conflict with Iran.
Diesel prices in Massachusetts increased nearly 79 cents per gallon, affecting delivery costs and goods prices.
New England's lack of refineries and pipeline access leaves the region vulnerable to global oil market fluctuations.

title: Patriots reportedly agree to 4-year contract with WR Romeo Doubs
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Patriots reportedly signed wide receiver Romeo Doubs to a four-year contract worth up to $80 million.
Doubs joins the Patriots after four seasons with the Green Bay Packers, where he had 55 catches for 724 yards in 2025.
The signing aims to enhance quarterback Drake Maye's options following the release of veteran receiver Stefon Diggs.

title: Airbnb is offering $750 to new hosts in World Cup cities, including Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Airbnb offers a $750 bonus to new hosts in World Cup cities, including Boston, for renting entire homes.
Boston World Cup hosts can earn an average of $5,200, second only to New York/New Jersey hosts with $5,700.
Searches for Airbnb stays in the 16 North American World Cup host cities have risen by 80% year-over-year.

title: Wellesley native running the Boston Marathon to honor family with Alzheimer’s
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Colin Rowan, 53, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer's Association MA/NH Chapter.
Rowan is a Massachusetts native whose grandmother and mother have suffered from dementia.
He aims to honor his family and raise awareness through his marathon participation.

title: Fundraiser launched for Reddit tipster who helped identify suspect in Brown shooting
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Fundraiser launched for Reddit tipster 'John' after helping identify suspect in Brown University shooting.
John's tip led investigators to Claudio Neves Valente, linked to December shootings at Brown and MIT.
Campaign by Ceremony cafe seeks $50,000 for John, who lacks permanent housing; $10,000 raised so far.

title: Joe Mazzulla praises Baylor Scheierman’s work ethic
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla praised Baylor Scheierman for his competitiveness and work ethic after victory over the Cavaliers.
Scheierman scored 16 points and had 10 rebounds in 26 minutes, continuing to play significant minutes post-Tatum's return.
Scheierman averages 4.8 points, 3.2 rebounds, and shoots 38.9% from three-point range over 59 games this season.

title: Police are searching for North Shore suspect in string of indecent assaults
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Revere police are searching for a suspect in multiple indecent assaults near the Beachmont MBTA Station between January and March.
The suspect is described as a Hispanic male, 5-foot-4 to 5-foot-7, thin build, with short dark hair.
Victims were approached from behind around 10-11 p.m.; residents asked to check surveillance footage and stay alert.

title: Wu announces millions in new funding for immigrant services
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu announced over $4.4 million in new funding for immigrant services in Boston.
Funds will support legal services, mental health, and basic necessities for immigrants, with contributions from city and philanthropic groups.
Wu's announcement aligns with efforts to limit ICE activities in Boston, following recent executive orders.

title: Report: Flag football game featuring Tom Brady moved out of Saudi Arabia
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Tom Brady's flag football game moved to Los Angeles from Saudi Arabia amid Middle East tensions.
The Fanatics Flag Football Classic will be held at Los Angeles' BMO Stadium on March 21.
Brady and Jalen Hurts to captain teams in a star-studded event featuring NFL veterans and current players.

title: Tell us: What’s been Joe Mazzulla’s best soundbite?
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla is known for his memorable soundbites during his stint in Boston.
Boston.com is seeking fan input to choose the best Joe Mazzulla soundbite for a March Madness-style bracket.
Mazzulla's quotes cover various topics, from team expectations to his favorite movie, showcasing his unique personality.
